Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages

Source: vignettes/using-in-other-packages.Rmd
using-in-other-packages.Rmd

Downstream packages use RsimdDispatch as a template and header provider. The usual workflow is:

  1. add RsimdDispatch to LinkingTo;
  2. copy the dispatch scaffold;
  3. replace the demo count_nonzero() kernels with package-specific kernels;
  4. keep CPU detection and dispatch files compiled with baseline flags;
  5. compile each ISA file with only the flags it needs.

DESCRIPTION

Add the header provider:

LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch

This makes bundled SIMDe headers available to C code:

#include <simde/x86/avx2.h>
#include <simde/arm/neon.h>

Copy the scaffold

From the downstream package root:

RsimdDispatch::use_simd_dispatch(".")

The copied scaffold includes:

configure
configure.win
cleanup
tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh
src/Makevars.in
src/cpu_features.c
src/simd_dispatch.c
src/kernel_scalar.c
src/kernel_avx2.c
src/kernel_avx512.c
src/kernel_neon.c

Replace the demo kernel

The demo kernel has this shape:

typedef size_t (*rsd_count_nonzero_fn)(const uint8_t *x, size_t n);

For a real package, rename the rsd_ and RC_ symbols and replace the kernel signature with your own. Keep the same structure:

R API wrapper        baseline flags
CPU feature checks   baseline flags
dispatch table       baseline flags
scalar kernel        baseline flags
SSE/AVX/NEON files   per-file ISA flags

Do not put -mavx2, -mavx512*, or -march=native in global package flags. The dispatcher must remain safe on baseline CPUs.

Build

The generated configure checks compiler support and writes src/config.h plus src/Makevars. Runtime CPU support is checked later by the installed package:

R CMD INSTALL .

On this page

Developed by Sounkou Mahamane Toure.
